headline: Ambulance Response Time Averages 22 Minutes in Urban Areas
context: Emergency ambulance response time in Malaysian cities significantly exceeds the international 8-minute standard.

cards.big: Average urban ambulance response time of 22 minutes nearly triple the 8-minute standard
cards.sub: Rural response times average 45 minutes with some areas exceeding 90 minutes

cards.big: Malaysia has only 1,400 ambulances for 33 million people or 4.2 per 100,000 population
cards.sub: International standard is 12-15 ambulances per 100,000 for adequate coverage

cards.big: Cardiac arrest survival drops 10% for every minute of delayed defibrillation
cards.sub: 22-minute average response means most cardiac arrest patients cannot be saved

cards.big: Dubai achieved 8-minute average response through GPS-dispatched motorcycle paramedic teams
cards.sub: First-responder motorcycle units bridge the gap before ambulance arrival

cards.big: In emergencies minutes equal lives—22-minute response times are a death sentence for many
Should Malaysia deploy motorcycle paramedic first-responders in all major urban areas
